response:
Certainly! Here's a professional and courteous resignation email template with a placeholder title:

---

**Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]**

Dear [Manager's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to formally resign from my position as [Your Position] at [Company Name], effective [Last Working Day, typically two weeks from the date of the email unless otherwise specified in your contract].

This was not an easy decision for me, as I have thoroughly enjoyed my time at [Company Name] and have greatly appreciated the opportunities for growth and development that the company has provided me. I am grateful for the support and guidance I have received from you and the entire team.

I am committed to ensuring a smooth transition. Over the coming weeks, I will do my best to tie up any loose ends, complete ongoing projects, and transfer my responsibilities to a suitable colleague. Please let me know how I can be of assistance during this transition period.

Thank you once again for the opportunity to be a part of [Company Name]. I have learned a great deal and have cherished my time working with such a talented and dedicated team. I wish the company continued success in all its endeavors.

Best regards,

[Your Full Name]  
[Your Position]  
[Your Contact Information]

---

Feel free to customize the placeholders to fit your specific situation.
